question_title: Will the average daily price of kerosene jet fuel in the US Gulf Coast be greater than $2.50 per gallon in March 2026?
summary: Current US Gulf Coast jet fuel prices (~$2.13-2.17/gal) are well below the $2.50 threshold, requiring a ~17% increase in about one month. Statistical analysis of historical month-over-month changes gives 3-8% probability depending on the distribution used. The fundamental outlook is strongly bearish: EIA forecasts Brent crude averaging $58/bbl for 2026 (currently ~$67-68), IEA warns of a record 3.7M bpd oil surplus, and WTI crude at ~$63 would need to reach $73-80 for jet fuel to hit $2.50. Only a major geopolitical shock or supply disruption could drive prices high enough, and even then, such a spike would need to be sustained for the entire month of March.

factors.description: Current jet fuel price (~$2.13-2.17/gal) is 15-17% below $2.50 threshold - a very large gap to close in ~1 month
EIA (Feb 2026 STEO) forecasts Brent crude averaging $58/bbl in 2026, with declining prices from current ~$67-68. WTI at $63 is well below the ~$73-80 needed for $2.50 jet fuel
IEA warns of record 3.7M bpd oil surplus in 2026, creating persistent downward pressure on oil and refined product prices
Statistical analysis: only 1 of 36 monthly changes exceeded the +$0.37 needed; normal distribution gives 3.1%, fat-tailed t(3) gives 8%
Jan 2026 monthly average was $2.031 and Feb is trending ~$2.13 - both declining from even these low levels relative to 2024 when March was $2.59
Geopolitical risk (Iran tensions, Middle East) and OPEC+ policy changes could cause sudden price spikes - tail risk factor

reasoning:

Key reasoning:

- **Current price gap is large**: Jet fuel is at ~$2.13-2.17/gal, needing a 15-17% jump to exceed $2.50
- **Oil fundamentals are bearish**: EIA forecasts Brent at $58/bbl avg for 2026 (declining from current $67-68), IEA warns of record 3.7M bpd surplus
- **Statistical rarity**: Only 1 of 36 historical month-over-month changes was large enough (+$0.37+); normal distribution gives 3.1% probability, fat-tailed 8%
- **Crude oil too low**: WTI at ~$63/bbl would need to reach ~$73-80 for $2.50 jet fuel
- **Tail risk caveat**: Geopolitical shock (Iran, Middle East) could spike prices, but would need to sustain through all of March
sources_consulted: US Gulf Coast jet fuel spot price 2025 2026
crude oil price forecast March 2026 WTI Brent outlook
